testcase.name: @stynx/sessions barrel > exports at least one symbol
SessionJwksController API contract > publishes the signing service JWKS payload
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > produces a 3-segment JWT signed by the active key
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > caches resolved keys across calls (singleton)
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > throws SessionSigningKeyError when activeKid is not present in keys
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > throws when neither jwt.keySet nor jwt.secretId is configured
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > throws when jwt.secretId is set but SecretLoader is unavailable
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > signs successfully when secret loader returns a valid key set
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > rejects when secret JSON is not an object
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > rejects when currentKid is missing or empty
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > rejects when keys array is missing or empty
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > rejects when a key entry is not an object
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > rejects when a key entry is missing kid/publicKeyPem/privateKeyPem
SessionJwtSigningService.signAccessToken > secretLoader path (validateKeySet) > preserves activatesAt + expiresAt when present
RedisSessionStore > requires initialization and closes only open clients
RedisSessionStore > creates, rotates, touches, revokes, indexes, and publishes sessions
RedisSessionStore > prunes stale ids from indexes
SessionService > creates, reads, and revokes a session
SessionService > rotates refresh tokens and kills the family when an old token is reused
SessionService > enforces absolute and idle timeout windows
SessionService > supports clock skew when access tokens have a slightly future nbf
SessionService > survives 1000 sequential refresh cycles without leaking live session records
SessionService > exchanges an active session for a new tenant-scoped session
SessionService > rejects tenant exchange for a mismatched actor
SessionService > rejects tenant exchange for a missing session
SessionService > rejects tenant exchange for an inactive session
SessionService > returns null or false for missing session operations and invalid refresh tokens
SessionService > rethrows non-expiry inactive session errors from get
SessionService > returns null for direct in-memory touch and rotate misses
SessionService > rejects refresh when lookup points at a missing session or rotation fails
SessionService > skips session mirroring when infrastructure providers are not available
SessionService > writes mirror rows with membership ids when infrastructure providers are available
SessionService > resolves optional audience and secret-id session options
SessionService > exchanges sessions without carrying optional device metadata or create metadata
SessionService > revokes all sessions for a user or tenant with one invalidation per affected scope
SessionService > does not publish tenant invalidation when no tenant sessions are active
